params.name: Well of Moses
params.description:
On Aug 6, 1403 AD, the Well of Moses first appeared in a monastery.
The Well of Moses is not a well, but a sculpture created by the sculptor Claus Sluter. Its shape is similar to a hexagonal pedestal, with a statue of a prophet from the Bible on each of the six sides. The characters in the sculpture are presented in a highly realistic manner, not only in their form but also conveying different ideas and emotions. It is considered one of the best sculptures of the Dutch Renaissance.
